description: Redesign do painel de contexto — substituir WebView por SwiftUI nativo. Design system Vesper dark, zero emojis, tipográfico.

userStories.title: Create design system constants and EventCardView
userStories.description: As a developer, I need a shared design system (colors, fonts) and the main event card component.
acceptanceCriteria: Create ErestorApp/Views/DesignSystem.swift with all color constants as static Color properties: surface=#1e1c1a, border=#2a2725, muted=#3d3733, dim=#4a4540, subtle=#6b5b50, text=#b8a99d, bright=#e0d5ca, green=#4a9e69, blue=#5b6d99, red=#c25a4a, amber=#c9a84c
Add dim variants: greenDim, blueDim, redDim, amberDim (8% opacity versions)
Define font helpers: mono (IBM Plex Mono via system monospaced), body (Inter via system default)

Create ErestorApp/Views/EventCardView.swift — SwiftUI view showing: colored vertical bar (2.5pt, green=work, blue=rest, gray=free) + event title (13pt bright) + time range (10.5pt subtle) + progress bar (2pt)
EventCardView accepts: title (String), timeRange (String), progress (Double 0-1), eventType (enum: work/rest/free)
Typecheck passes
userStories.notes: Reference prototype: ~/projetos/erestor/prototipo-painel.html (CSS classes .ev, .ev-r, .ev-b, .ev-c, .ev-t, .ev-m, .ev-p, .ev-f). Colors from :root CSS vars. The panel width is 288pt on desktop. Use @Environment or passed bindings for data.

userStories.title: Create TimerChipView and NextEventView
userStories.description: As a user, I want to see my active timer and what's coming next.
acceptanceCriteria: Create ErestorApp/Views/TimerChipView.swift — green chip with: timer text (IBM Plex Mono 14pt green), label (10.5pt muted green), stop button (mono 10pt, turns red on hover)
Chip has greenDim background + green border (15% opacity) + 8pt corner radius
TimerChipView accepts: elapsed (String like '47:12'), label (String), onStop (() -> Void)
Create ErestorApp/Views/NextEventView.swift — horizontal row: time remaining (mono 10pt dim, min 32pt) + title (11pt dim, truncated)
NextEventView accepts: timeRemaining (String like '2h15'), title (String)
Typecheck passes
userStories.notes: Reference: .tc and .nxt CSS classes in prototype. TimerChipView sits inside EventCardView section (visually nested under the event). NextEventView has a separator line above it.

userStories.title: Create TaskListView, PollCardView, and GateAlertView
userStories.description: As a user, I want to see my P1 tasks, respond to polls, and see gate alerts in the panel.
acceptanceCriteria: Create ErestorApp/Views/TaskListView.swift — vertical list of tasks, each with: red dot (4pt) + task text (11.5pt #8a7d73)
TaskListView accepts: tasks ([String])
Create ErestorApp/Views/PollCardView.swift — card with dark background (#242120), border, 10pt radius, slideIn animation
PollCardView has two modes: energy (5 level buttons with number+label) and quality (4 text buttons: perdi/meh/ok/flow)
Poll buttons highlight on tap (green border+bg), then card fades out after 400ms
PollCardView accepts: type (energy/quality), question (String), onResponse ((String) -> Void)
Create ErestorApp/Views/GateAlertView.swift — card with amber or red tint, text + 2 action buttons
GateAlertView accepts: text (String), severity (amber/red), actions ([(label: String, action: () -> Void)])
Typecheck passes
userStories.notes: Reference: .tasks, .poll, .gate CSS classes in prototype. Poll energy has 5 buttons (1-morto, 2-baixa, 3-ok, 4-boa, 5-pico). Quality has 4 (perdi, meh, ok, flow). Gate has from label 'erestor · gate' and 2 buttons. Use withAnimation for slideIn and fade effects.

userStories.title: Create ChatInputView with expandable history
userStories.description: As a user, I want a chat input always visible at the bottom, with history expanding upward when focused.
acceptanceCriteria: Create ErestorApp/Views/ChatInputView.swift — text field in a rounded container (#242120 bg, border, 10pt radius)
Input placeholder 'pergunte algo...' in muted color, text in bright color, 11.5pt Inter
Send button (arrow up) in muted, becomes subtle on hover
ChatInputView accepts: onSend ((String) -> Void)
Create ErestorApp/Views/ChatHistoryView.swift — scrollable list of messages (user=subtle, bot=text color)
Each message has role label (mono 9pt muted) + text (11.5pt)
History has max height 180pt, hidden by default, expands when input is focused or has messages
ChatHistoryView accepts: messages ([ChatMessage])
Typecheck passes
userStories.notes: Reference: .cbox, .crow, .cin, .cgo, .chat-m CSS classes in prototype. History expands upward with animation (max-height transition). The chat functionality already exists in ChatService — this just needs to call chatService.sendMessageStreaming().

userStories.title: Create ContextPanelView composing all subviews
userStories.description: As a developer, I need a main panel view that composes all the sub-components and receives data from the API.
acceptanceCriteria: Create ErestorApp/Views/ContextPanelView.swift — VStack composing (top to bottom): header (dot + time), EventCardView, TimerChipView (if active), separator, NextEventView, separator, TaskListView, dynamic cards (PollCardView/GateAlertView), ChatInputView at bottom
Panel has surface background, 14pt corner radius, 1px border, shadow
Header shows: green/gray status dot (5pt) + time (mono 10pt subtle) + close button (x)
ContextPanelView observes ChatService for messages and streaming state
ContextPanelView has a @Published contextData property fed from /api/context JSON
Map API fields to subviews: current_event→EventCardView, timer→TimerChipView, next_event→NextEventView, active_p1s→TaskListView
Dynamic cards appear/disappear based on push events (poll_energy, poll_quality, gate_inform)
Typecheck passes
userStories.notes: The /api/context response structure is in ChatService.swift (ContextSummary). Push events come through ChatService push polling. The panel width should be 288pt (matching prototype). Use @StateObject or @ObservedObject for ChatService.

userStories.title: Replace WebView chat panel with SwiftUI ContextPanelView
userStories.description: As a user, I want the native SwiftUI panel instead of the WebView-based one.
acceptanceCriteria: BubbleWindowController.createChatPanel() creates an NSHostingView with ContextPanelView instead of WKWebView
Remove ChatWebViewController and ChatWebViewVC dependency from BubbleWindowController
Keep the chat.html file for now (fallback) but don't load it
Context polling feeds the new SwiftUI panel via ChatService.context property
Stream observation pushes tokens to ChatHistoryView instead of JavaScript calls
Push event handling (poll/gate/reminder) creates dynamic cards in ContextPanelView
Keyboard shortcuts still work: Escape closes, Cmd+K clears
Notification dot still works (show on stream finish / push with chat closed)
Typecheck passes
userStories.notes: This is the integration story. BubbleWindowController currently uses ChatWebViewVC (WKWebView) and evaluateJavaScript calls. Replace with NSHostingView wrapping ContextPanelView. The ChatService already has all the data — just need to bind it to SwiftUI. Keep the WebView code commented out (not deleted) in case of rollback.
